content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 21, 1990

Robert E. Weiss, Treasurer
Californians for a Citizen Government
920 Village Oaks Drive
P.O. Box 3269
Covina, CA 91722

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of
Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-90-678

Dear Mr. Weiss:

This letter is in response to your correspondence of
October 25, 1990, in which you memorialize the telephone advice
provided to you on that date regarding the truth in initiative
advertising provision of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹
This letter contains general guidance as to the disclosure
requirements of the Act; we consider it to be informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed).²

Your letter accurately reflects the advice provided to you.
Under the provisions of Section 84507(b), a committee which
authorizes and pays for an advertisement which supports or opposes
a statewide initiative must disclose the person³ whose contri-
butions to the committee are one hundred thousand dollars or more
and who is the largest contributor to the committee. A committee
must disclose the person³ whose contri-

______
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

3 For purposes of the truth in initiative advertising provisions
of the Act, "person" means any individual, business, and any other
organization or group of persons acting in concert. (Section
84505.)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. I-90-678
Page 2


can constitute a major funding source for another committee which
has authorized an advertisement, in which case the name of the
contributing committee must be disclosed when the appropriate
contribution threshold has been reached. (Hiltachk Advice Letter,
No. A-90-336, copy enclosed.) This advice was premised on the
understanding that the two committees were separate and distinct
entities.

     Pursuant to the above, you were advised that the statement
"Major Funding - The Schabarum Fund for California's Future" would
satisfy the requirements of the Act. Additionally, as you correctly state in your letter, this statement must be voiced.

     Please note, however, that disclosure of the name of a
committee as a major funding source may not always be sufficient
for purposes of Section 84507. This would be the case, for
example, if the contributing committee is established for the same
general policy purpose and its activities are managed and
conducted by the same individuals as the committee authorizing the
advertisement. In such circumstances, the two committees are
separate and distinct in name only. Disclosure of the
contributing committee as a major funding source is also not
sufficient if that committee is functioning as an intermediary.
In either of these situations, contributors to such contributing
committee and the amounts they contribute must be examined to
determine the major funding source. (Mulholland Advice Letter,
No. A-90-552, copy enclosed.)

     We are in the process of drafting regulations to implement
the provisions of Proposition 105. Consequently, the advice you
have been provided in this letter may change if the Commission
adopts regulations which reach a different result.

     We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel


                                        By: Blanca M. Breeze
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:aa

Enclosures
